feed.title: Accessibility Wars - paragon
feed.subtitle: Accessible Builds for the Guild Wars 2 Community

entry.title: I'll Never Yield [pDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This raid build focuses on utilizing Warrior's strong Dual Wielding trait on Paragon, allowing for a selfless low intensity DPS that's great for group content.

It requires the Weaponmaster Training from Secrets of the Obscure to be able to wield the dagger as Paragon.

It also has high burst APM, as there is a 5 button combo that you ideally press quickly. Expect lower DPS if you do not press this combo quickly.

Consider it a more selfless alternative to the Magebane Daggerbreaker build.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Dagger Berserker's Force
Mace Berserker's Air
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Claw
  • Food:  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak or  Bowl of Sweet and Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone

Standard Build

[&DQIEHyQ2SidwAHAAdh12Ha8ArwCpAKkA7gDuAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADLwA1AAUAAA==]

Rotation

At the start of the fight, use all of the signets you have, saving Signet of Fury for right when you are about to attack the boss.

  1. Breaching Strike (Dagger F1)
  2. Signet of Fury (won't be available in opener)
  3. Chant of Action (F2) (only if it is not active or your motivation is less than 7)
  4. Crushing Blow (Mace 4)
  5. Tremor (Mace 5)
  6. Crushing Blow (Mace 4)
  7. Auto until F1 is up again
  8. Breaching Strike (Dagger F1)
  9. Disrupting Stab (Dagger 3)
  10. Auto until Mace 5 has less than 2 seconds left on its cooldown

Notes

Relic

Upkeep Relic of the Claw with Tremor (Mace 5) and Disrupting Stab (Dagger 3). The rotation is made in such a way that you are reaching ~90% uptime of Claw without much thought. For slightly higher Claw uptime, delay your Dagger 3 a little bit instead of doing it immediately after your Dagger F1.

You can also use Relic of the Eagle for a DPS loss of ~3k.

Weapon Set 2 options

Dagger / Axe for more DPS but higher APM

Your rotation becomes:

  1. Breaching Strike (Dagger F1)
  2. Signet of Fury (won't be available in opener)
  3. Chant of Action (F2) (if it is not active or your motivation is less than 7)
  4. Crushing Blow (Mace 4)
  5. Tremor (Mace 5)
  6. Crushing Blow (Mace 4)
  7. Weapon swap
  8. Dual Strike (Axe 4)
  9. Whirling Axe (Axe 5)
  10. Auto until F1 is up again
  11. Breaching Strike (Dagger F1)
  12. Disrupting Stab (Dagger 3)
  13. Weapon swap
  14. Auto until Mace 5 has 2 seconds left on its cooldown
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Dagger Berserker's Force
Axe Berserker's Hydromancy

Remember you don't need to buy a second Dagger; just equip the Axe offhand.

Spear for CC

When you need to do big CC, swap to Spear and use skills 3 and 5. Swap back once CC is done.

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Spear Berserker's Hydromancy
Paralyzation

Chants

Your F2's refrain is what you will be camping most of the time. However, don't forget your other chants.

Most notably, Chant of Freedom is a group stun break and also gives stability. To use it, simply press it, then immediately use your F2 again; it's a 15% DPS loss to forget to head back to your F2 chant, so make sure to do so.

Utilities and Elites

Signet of Fury is the only utility skill truly required for this build, as removing all of the other Signets results in only a 3k DPS loss. When the build already is getting ~35k, this is more than acceptable if you're willing to bring utility for your team. Discuss with your allies if there's anything that needs covered, such as CC, Resistance, Stability, etc.

As far as what to replace, always keep your Signet of Fury as it's a big DPS loss to remove it. Removing Signet of Might is also a DPS loss, but if your team needs extra help it's still worth taking off.

Elites

For your elites, you have two options:

Would you like to resurrect downed people, up to five, almost instantly? Battle Standard is an easy choice and is the usual default; it's hard to wrong here.

Would you like to prevent mistakes from ever happening to begin with, or ignore mechanics that would otherwise down the whole group? Feel confident in timing it correctly? "We Will Never Yield!" is your new best friend. Some places you can bring it include:

  • Sunqua Peak CM (for all of the spread mechanics)
  • Conjured Amalgamate (for the clap)
  • Cardinal Sabir (ignoring tornadoes / "uppies" and "corners")
  • Deimos CM (for a guaranteed chill Mind Crush in case Aegis gets stripped, such as if oils were spread at the same time as Mind Crush)

Utilities

Consider for your utilities:

  • Throw Bolas for long-range immobilization.
  • Kick, Bull's Charge, "On Your Knees!" and Banner of Strength for more CC.
  • Banner of Tactics for superspeed, stability, resistance and group stun break.
  • "Never Surrender!" for resistance, aegis, resolution, and group stun break.
  • Banner of Defense for barrier or aegis.
  • "Brace Yourselves!" for barrier.
  • Endure Pain or Defiant Stance for ignoring damage, allowing you to skip mechanics like Deimos's Mind Crush ("Stand in the ward!").

Video

Changes

content/builds/power-paragon.md was changed:

  • Hushblade: Swipe out in a cone before you, dazing enemies struck.

entry.title: Sword Saint Commander [aHeal]
entry.content:
Notes

This is a build which uses Celestial gear and hybrid traits to maximise the sword's physical prowress and bleeding capabilities. This build is designed to replace a mod-high toughness dedicated healer slot. Compared to other celestial healers, this build has one of the lower damage ceilings and is less preferred in the meta. However, it is favored on this site due to the easily achievable high damage floor (14k Sw/WH camp).

Hybrid healing builds have a lot of give and take and toe the line to bring 'just enough', so you need to adjust the build to the strengths of your team composition.

Gearing

EquipmentStatRune/Sigil
Hvy ArmorCelestial Superior Rune of Divinity
Sword (MH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Torment
Sword (OH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Earth
All TrinketsCelestialN/A
Relic (DPS)N/ARelic of the Fractal
Relic (Heal)N/ARelic of the Flock
Relic (Sust)N/A-

Optional weapon sets

EquipmentStatSigil
Warhorn (OH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Earth
Mace (OH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Earth
Dagger (OH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Earth
Shield (OH)Celestial Superior Sigil of Paralyzation

Food, Enhancement

  • Food:  Spherified Cilantro Oyster Soup
  • Enhancement:  Magnanimous Tuning Crystal
  • It is possible to mix other stats to increase DPS but you want to at least maintain 1000 healing power and 40% boon duration.
  • Try to maintain at least 10k DPS or you may opt to do the reverberation healing build instead.
  • If you ever find yourself switching to staff, play a dedicated healer instead.
  • Food and utilities with universal stats do very well
    1.  Decade Enhancement Station
    2.  Snow Diamond Ornament
    3. Fractal Cooking/Sharpening Golem

Build

DPS Group Content

[&DQIkNQsuShZ5HQAAqAAAAK0BAAB+HQAA7gAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]
  • Blademaster provides a higher DPS but Furious is better if you are sitting on Warhorn for long periods.
  • Reverberation is a very powerful flexible support trait with minimal DPS loss.

Rotation

Opener

Your ability to perform the opener will depend on the boss and you may need to swap the order of the skills.

  1. Commands
    • "Brace Yourselves!" for adrenaline
    • "Never Surrender!" for boons
  2. Chant Skills
    • Chant of Action
    • Chant of Recuperation
    • Chant of Freedom
  3. Use warhorn skills
    • Charge
    • Call of Valor
  4. Weapon swap ⚔ for DPS
  • Your Chant skills provide alacrity and boons, you need to keep swapping between them to reduce their own cooldowns and maintain boons.
  • Periodically swap to warhorns for extra barrier and swiftness.

DPS rotation

Use the following off-cooldown

  1. Bloodthirster (⚔ Burst)
  2. Impale (⚔ 4 Optional)
  3. Rend (⚔ 3)
  • If you need CC, using mace in the offhand shortens your DPS rotation with only minimal loss in DPS.
  • While swapped to warhorn, Savage Leap may result in a DPS increase.

General House Rules

  1. Your primary goal is to maintain boons, alacrity and your party's survivability before DPS
  2. You need to maintain 8-10 boons and 100% alacrity uptime.

Crowd Control

Reverberation and "On Your Knees!" provides a solid AOE defiance break, (eg. shields on CA) such that you will rarely require anything else. Additional options are listed here for posterity:

  • You may carry off-hand mace, dagger or shield for additional hard CC.
  • Take off-hand dagger if you require a boon strip.
  • Other utilities with defiance breaks may be picked with caution.

Latest patch changes

  • The new changes to dagger allows it to be used for both boon-strip and CC.
  • Sword no longer scales more towards condi but the build still retains its high damage floor.

entry.title: Vocals of Eternity [aHeal]
entry.content:
Notes

Its quite hard to do badly on this build... This build gambles on Reverberation to empower Commands.

Gearing

EquipmentStatRune/Sigil
Hvy ArmorHarrier's Superior Rune of the Monk
Staff (2H)Harrier's Superior Sigil of Transference
-- Superior Sigil of Concentration
Dagger (MH)Harrier's Superior Sigil of Water
Warhorn (OH)Harrier's Superior Sigil of Renewal
All TrinketsMinstrel'sN/A
RelicN/A Relic of the Flock

Food, Enhancement

  • Food:  Bowl of Fruit Salad with Mint Garnish
  • Enhancement:  Bountiful Maintenance Oil

Optional weapon sets

You may replace Dagger/warhorn

Main Hand

  • Mace (for Tanking and CC)

Off Hand

  • Mace (for CC)
  • Dagger (Boon Strip and CC)

Build

GW2Skills Link

[&DQILHzM/Sh55HQAAhh0AAH4dAAB2HQAAcx0AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]

Rotation

Other than providing alacrity, there isn't a real rotation, you could mash all the buttons on cooldown and it will heal, provide 10-11 boons and bruteforce heal/barrier most mechanics.

However, the build has a lot of room for optimisation and it is important to reserve some skills for when you require them. The situations below are non-exhaustive and you will find more uses that will not be covered in this guide. However because there are many options, you will still perform well if you mess up badly or do not follow the rotation described below.

Opener

The opener is optional and helps to speed up initial ramp of adrenaline and prioritises might uptime.

  1. Use all your commands to build adrenaline.
  2. Chant of Action (F2)_
  3. Path to Victory (F1)
  4. 🔁Weapon swap (to Warhorn) and use WH skills

    Charge Call of Valor

  5. Rotate your chants to provide alacrity.
  6. 🔁Weapon swap (to Staff)

Providing Alacrity

Alternate your chants every 6s to provide alacrity. Use Valiant Leap (⚕️2) and Defiant Roar (⚕️5) to get adrenaline. For Chants, there is a certain priority depending on the situation:

SkillKeyWhen prioritised
Chant of ActionF2To give might and fury
Chant of RecuperationF3When you need heals
Chant of FreedomF4When you need stability/stun-break

Commands and Other Boons

You must use your commands to grant boons, but you must also reserve some for emergencies.

SkillEffect
"We Will Never Yield!"Used to bypass raid mechanics
"We Shall Return!"Lots of healing and condi-cleanses
"Brace Yourselves!"Barrier
"Find Their Weakness!"For Might uptime
"Never Surrender!"For Aegis, Resistance, Resolution
  • "Find Their Weakness!" and "Never Surrender!" should be used as part of your rotation to maintain boons.
  • Many of your other boons are covered by your weapons and there is some overlap.

Crowd Control

Command CC

"On Your Knees!" is a very strong pulsing CC that is ample in almost all situations. Use it to replace "Find Their Weakness!"

Weapon skills that provide breakbar damage

  1. Disrupting Stab (🗡️3)
  2. Snap Pull (⚕️4)
  3. Skull Crack (🏏F1)
  4. Tremor (🏏5)
  5. Pommel Bash (🏏3) (only when tanking)

CC Utilities
Additional CC options are available but reduce your might uptime when you switch out another command to use them. I do not recommend doing so and hence it will not be covered in the guide.

entry.title: Dual Blademaster [cDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This is a build which uses mostly Vipers Gear to maximise the dual swords new reworked bleeding capabilities.

This build has a lot of auto attacks.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Viper's Trapper
Grieving Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
EquipmentStatRune/Sigil
Sword (MH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Torment
Sword (OH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Earth
All TrinketsViper'sN/A
Relic (DPS)N/ARelic of the Fractal
Relic (DPS)N/ARelic of the Thorn
Relic (Sust)N/ARelic of the Biomancer
Relic (Sust)N/ARelic of the Mistfire

Optional equipment sets

EquipmentStatSigil
Mace (OH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Earth
Dagger (OH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Earth
  • Note: Sinister stats can be used in place of the one Grieving piece.

Food, Enhancement

  • Food:  Spherified Cilantro Oyster Soup
  • Food:  Dragon's Revelry Starcake (Budget)
  • Food:  Feast of Delectable Birthday Cake (Budget)
  • Enhancement:  Toxic Tuning Crystal
  • Better (prohibitively expensive) food/enhancements are available
    • Snow Diamond Ornament
    •  Writ of Masterful Malice
    •  Writ of Masterful Accuracy
    • Cooking Golem and/or Sharpening Golem (may be freely available on occasion)

Infusions

  •  Malign WvW Infusion can be used for all empty slots (optional)

Build

Viper's Variant

[&DQIEHyQ+SiWmAAAAswAAAK8AAACpAAAAnAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]

Rotation

Opener

  1. Signets (Cast very early to maintain 5 stacks)
  2. Chant of Action
  3. "To the Limit!"
  • None of these skills will trigger the boss fight, and you can perpetually keep refreshing signet until the fight begins.
  • Signet of Fury and "To the Limit!" provide adrenaline so you may use them in between fight phases or as part of your opener

Skill Priority

Use the following off-cooldown

  1. Bloodthirster (⚔ Burst)
  2. Impale (⚔ 4)
  3. Rend (⚔ 3)
  4. Dolyak Signet
  5. Signet of Might (if required)
  6. Other Signets only if required
  • Try to maintain 4-5 stacks of Signet Mastery.
  • Dolyak Signet has no cast time.
  • Signet of Might has no cast time.

Crowd Control

This build has very poor CC options and you may bring additional CC by sacrificing Dolyak Signet but Signet Mastery will be harder to maintain. Other weapon sets provide decent CC with minimal DPS loss.

  1. "On Your Knees!"
  2. Kick
  3. Tremor (swap to mace)
  4. Hushblade (swap to dagger)

Sustain in Open World

  1. Switch to Healing Signet for baseline healing and to maintain Signet Mastery
  2. Use Relic of the Biomancer for baseline healing
  3. Use Relic of the Mistfire for life siphon and additional damage
  4. Use "We Will Never Yield!" in emergencies
  5. Use Dolyak Signet for stability

Latest patch changes

  • Sword has been reworked. The grieving variant has been removed from the guide as sword no longer scales well to power.
  • Dagger has been added as a new CC option. This is useful as dagger offers boon-strip as well.
